Commercial Apr 6, 2026

“There’s A Huge Demand”: The Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner Demolished After Only 13 Flying Hours

A barely flown Boeing 787-8 is scrapped because its parts are worth more than the whole.

Supply chain disruptions and parts scarcity have created perverse economic incentives where aircraft cannibalization becomes more profitable than operational deployment.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The Cost Of Flying Premium Economy In 2026 Compared To Economy

The hidden truth about premium economy.

Premium economy pricing power is eroding as carriers compete for middle-market passengers, forcing fleet reconfiguration decisions that impact aircraft utilization economics.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The World's Largest Airline By Number Of Hubs

American Airlines reigns supreme on this front.

American Airlines' hub-centric network strategy creates competitive advantages in connectivity and market reach, but also concentrates operational risk and increases vulnerability to disruptions at key facilities.

See Simple Flying →

China tests heavy cargo drone designed for extreme terrain

China has completed the maiden flight of a new 7-tonne autonomous cargo aircraft, taking a...

China's autonomous heavy-lift drone development signals a strategic push to establish cargo delivery capabilities in remote regions where traditional aviation infrastructure is limited.

See sUAS News →

Safety Apr 6, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Strong SMS effectiveness depends less on compliance processes than on psychological safety enabling frontline employees to report hazards without fear of retaliation.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Not So Fast: How The Boeing 757 Made A Comeback At This Luxury Airline

The aircraft is surprisingly rejoining the airline's fleet.

The 757's return demonstrates renewed demand for fuel-efficient, long-range narrowbodies on profitable leisure routes where legacy aircraft still outperform newer alternatives.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight

Four astronauts aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight around the Moon made history at 12:56 p.m. CDT on Monday, traveling 248,655 miles from Earth, surpassing the record for human spaceflight’s farthest distance previously set by the Apollo 13 mission in 1970. At its farthest point, crew inside the...

Artemis II's achievement validates NASA's deep-space exploration architecture, demonstrating critical operational capabilities for sustained lunar missions and future Mars expeditions.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Downsizing: Allegiant Air To Close Bellingham & Savannah Crew Bases In November

As Allegiant Air prepares to close its Bellingham and Savannah crew bases, questions arise about the future of smaller airport hubs.

Allegiant's base closures signal a strategic retreat from smaller markets, potentially signaling broader industry consolidation pressures on ultra-low-cost carriers' regional network viability.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview

NASA’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24, will deliver approximately 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This mission will be the second flight of the Cygnus XL, the larger, more cargo-capable version of the...

The Cygnus XL's increased 11,000-pound payload capacity expands ISS resupply flexibility and reduces mission frequency needed to maintain station operations.

See NASA →

Safety Apr 6, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why selected controls are adequate rather than eliminating all theoretical risks, balancing operational feasibility with safety outcomes.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

K-State Salina Launches High School Aviation Camp

Weeklong program will include simulator work, ground school and up to three hours of flight time.

Kansas State's structured pipeline program combines theoretical and practical flight training to cultivate early interest in aviation careers during critical high school years.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Moog Highlights Growing Satellite Bus Capabilities with Full‑Scale METEOR Reveal at Space Symposium

East Aurora, NY — Moog Inc. (NYSE: MOG.A and MOG.B), a worldwide designer, manufacturer and systems integrator of high-performance precision motion and fluid controls and control systems will highlight its […]...

Moog's modular METEOR satellite bus platform positions the company to capture growing demand from commercial space operators seeking faster deployment and cost-efficient spacecraft production.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities

Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is pushing into Japan’s defense market after securing a local partner to expand its reach, building on a shift beyond tiny picosatellites used to connect low-power monitoring devices toward more capable spacecraft for broader government applications.

FOSSA's evolution from picosatellites to defense-grade smallsats signals a strategic pivot toward government contracts, potentially creating new satellite communication infrastructure opportunities for aerospace suppliers and integrators.

See SpaceNews →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Air Tractor Holdings Acquires Thrush Aircraft

Deal combines two ag aviation manufacturers under shared ownership.

The consolidation creates a more vertically integrated ag-aviation competitor with expanded production capacity and complementary aircraft portfolios to compete against larger diversified manufacturers.

See AVweb →

▶
General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model supporting flight training alongside regional air carrier operations demonstrates a viable hub strategy for mid-sized Midwestern airports facing competitive pressures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Hartzell Expands Top Prop Program

Update adds 150+ models, pricing changes for GA aircraft upgrades.

Hartzell's expanded propeller portfolio now covers 150+ additional aircraft models, enabling broader retrofit opportunities across the GA fleet and potentially shifting maintenance costs for operators.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The Main Features That Made The Boeing 777 A Long-Haul Leader

Why the Boeing 777 is the most successful widebody airliner of all time.

The 777's design pioneered fly-by-wire technology for widebodies and twin-engine economics that enabled profitable ultra-long-range routes previously requiring four engines.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

How Regional Turboprop Aircraft Are Making A Comeback

Props are soaring back up the charts.

Regional turboprops' resurgence is driven by fuel efficiency and lower operating costs as airlines seek alternatives to costlier regional jets amid sustained high fuel prices.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Poll: Will Phoenix Trigger Broader Landing Fees?

Could Phoenix-area landing fees spark broader GA change?

Phoenix's landing fee experiment may force GA airports nationwide to reconsider revenue models as financial pressures intensify post-pandemic.

Safety Apr 6, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure at Reno-Tahoe requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that differ from standard departures at the airport.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Landing Fees and Flight Training in Phoenix

As Falcon Field and Mesa Gateway add landing fees for light aircraft, officials and flight schools describe different futures for the Phoenix area.

Phoenix-area flight schools face potential operational cost increases and student demand shifts as nearby airports implement landing fees, potentially consolidating training activity to fewer facilities.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Just 4 Hours: United Airlines' 10 New Shortest Transatlantic Flights Revealed

Two of the routes are new or returning this year. Discover them and much more!

United's new short-haul transatlantic routes exploit underserved city pairs, potentially pressuring legacy carriers' profitable long-haul networks.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Here’s How Much Fuel The Boeing 787 Dreamliner Saves Compared To Older Jets

The 787 Dreamliner was a first-of-its-kind aircraft. One that moved beyond what was considered the norm for an airliner, breaking that tradition.

The 787's advanced composite construction and aerodynamic design reduce fuel consumption by approximately 20-25% versus comparable wide-body aircraft, directly improving airline profitability and environmental impact.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II

Today, the crew of Artemis II reaches a milestone, traveling farther than any humans in recorded history, as they orbit the far side of the Moon, roughly 4,000 miles above the lunar surface. You can watch this historic broadcast on NASA’s YouTube channel today, April 6, 2026, at 10 AM Pacific (1 PM...

Artemis II's trans-lunar trajectory demonstrates NASA's validated deep-space navigation capabilities, establishing operational precedent for sustained lunar missions and future Mars exploration architecture.

See NASA →

Industry Apr 6, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impacts as traffic demand continues accelerating.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Grants

Grants Status Requests To submit a request, visit NASA General Information Request Form and complete the form. You will receive an automated email with the most commonly requested grant status information. Important Instructions: How to Fill Out the Form: Memorandum for NASA Grantee Community...

NASA's streamlined grant status request process through automated forms reduces administrative burden on aerospace researchers and contractors seeking funding updates.

See NASA →

Military Apr 6, 2026

Lady of Larissa Flies Again: Incredible New Footage Emerges of the Secretive RQ-180 Spy Drone

New videos give an even clearer view – unquestionably our best ever – of the legendary classified stealth drone, previously seen landing in Greece last month. Last month, we reported on images taken in Larissa, Greece, showing what we, and others, concluded – based on prior sightings and comment...

Recent high-definition footage of the RQ-180 stealth drone provides unprecedented visual confirmation of a classified aircraft previously known only through fragmentary intelligence reports.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The Near Side of the Moon

The astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft captured this photo of the Moon’s near side on April 4, 2026. The image features dark patches at center and right; these are ancient lava flows, which are unique to this side of the Moon. The Artemis II astronauts – astronauts Victor Glover, Reid...

Artemis II's lunar imagery documents compositional differences between Moon hemispheres, informing resource mapping for sustained human exploration missions.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Amazon Leo to serve as launchpad for more Delta brand experiences

The proverbial ‘gravitational pull’ of low-latency Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-powered inflight connectivity is continuing unabated in commercial aviation, as Delta Air Lines earmarks Amazon Leo for an initial 500 tails from 2028. Announced on 31 March, the arrangement springboards off the US...

Delta's commitment to Amazon's LEO satellite network across 500 aircraft signals operator confidence that low-latency connectivity will become a competitive differentiator by 2028.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Apr 6, 2026

USAF F-16s Flying With AGM-158 JASSMs in Operation Epic Fury Against Iran

Long-range JASSM strikes still continue on Iranian targets, particularly against missile and drone production facilities. U.S. Air Force F-16s currently deployed in support of Operation Epic Fury have been shown in official photos carrying the AGM-158 Joint-Air-to-Surface Standoff Missile (JASSM)...

JASSM deployment demonstrates F-16s' evolving role in sustained standoff strike operations, extending conventional deterrence capabilities against distributed target sets.

See The Aviationist →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

National STOL Announces 2026 Competition Schedule

Series plans eight events across the U.S. beginning in April.

Eight-event STOL circuit expansion signals growing competitive momentum in short-takeoff-and-landing aircraft market for backcountry operators.

Airlines are in a fierce competition for top talent, and pilots are reaping the rewards.

Pilot compensation has become a critical retention tool as carriers compete for experienced crew amid ongoing staffing pressures and industry growth.

Drones Apr 6, 2026

Invisible drone defense helped secure the massive JUNO Awards crowd

When tens of thousands of fans gather under one roof, security doesn’t just stop at the doors; it extends to the skies above. That was exactly the case at Canada’s biggest night in music, the JUNO Awards, where a quiet but critical layer of anti-drone protection was keeping watch overhead. more…...

Counter-drone technology is becoming standard infrastructure at major public events, reflecting growing security concerns about airspace threats in crowded venues.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.

Early in my career at SpaceX, I was the only person dedicated to training the mission control team for Dragon. For three years, I built the training for the operations team. The documentation came second, always the thing I’d get to later, always the thing that felt like overhead against the actual...

Lunar base operations planning risks repeating SpaceX's early mistake of prioritizing hardware over software documentation and training infrastructure, which could compromise mission reliability and crew safety.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Press Release: Newly rebranded Aeroplay to unveil Nucleus OS

Aeroplay Entertainment, a leading Content Service Provider (CSP) in the aviation industry, is entering an exciting new chapter. The company is rebranding under is Passenger Experience Partner vision to deliver more integrated and passenger-centric inflight solutions. This transformation is...

Aeroplay's pivot to passenger experience integration signals consolidation in inflight entertainment toward holistic cabin systems rather than standalone content delivery.

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha

St. Petersburg, FL — April 6, 2026 — Seagate Space Corporation announced today a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Firefly Aerospace to collaborate on the development of an offshore launch platform […]...

Offshore launch infrastructure partnerships signal industry movement toward distributed launch sites that could reduce terrestrial congestion and expand payload capacity access.
